title: Heuchera nana
taxonomy: Heuchera nana is a species of Heuchera, a genus of flowering plants in the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae). Heuchera nana is native to North America. It belongs to the tribe Heuchereae and is often referred to as the dwarf coral bells. The plant is characterized by its maple-like leaves and small white flowers. Heuchera nana is a popular ornamental plant due to its unique foliage and compact growth habit.

intro: Heuchera nana, also known as Alumroot or Dwarf Heuchera, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its small, lobed leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and fever. Bioactive constituents of Heuchera nana include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
